steps.title: Spot the logical gap
steps.content: The scientists say known benefits seem insufficient to explain why deep sleep—which makes animals vulnerable—became so widespread. The key signal: a risky behavior that's too widespread to be explained by known benefits alone.

steps.title: The answer that resolves it
steps.content:
Choice A claims deep sleep has undiscovered advantages—the only way to account for widespread adoption despite insufficient known benefits.

  • B generalizes to all traits
  • C shifts focus away from function
  • D frames it as a generic benefit-risk trade-off
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
